message:
feat(workflows/context): tick 213 WorkflowContext::send_*_with_cx siblings (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

WorkflowContext had 4 pub async send methods — send_text, send_ctrl_c,
send_ctrl_d, send_ctrl_z — with no cx-first siblings. These are the
primary surface by which Workflow impls dispatch to the host pane
via the policy gate.

The downstream cx-first paths exist:
- PolicyEngine::send_text_with_cx (policy.rs L6346)
- PolicyEngine::send_ctrl_c_with_cx (policy.rs L6672)
- PolicyEngine::send_ctrl_d_with_cx (policy.rs L6696)
- PolicyEngine::send_ctrl_z_with_cx (policy.rs L6720)
- runtime_compat::Mutex::lock_with_cx (injector async lock)

Tick 213 adds 4 `*_with_cx` siblings to WorkflowContext. Each
mirrors its legacy twin but:
1. Uses `injector.lock_with_cx(cx)` instead of `.lock().await` so
   the async Mutex acquire observes cancel.
2. Calls `guard.send_*_with_cx(cx, ...)` so the inner wezterm
   dispatch threads cx via the policy engine's cx-first inject path.

Legacy send_text/send_ctrl_c/send_ctrl_d/send_ctrl_z preserved
verbatim.

Workflow impls that want fine-grained cancellation in their step
bodies can now call `ctx.send_text_with_cx(cx, ...)` instead of
`ctx.send_text(...)`. Matches the pattern noted in tick 187 as the
next step beyond the trait-level execute_step_cx default — this
gives per-impl workflows a concrete cx-first surface to migrate to.

Verification: 1168 / 1168 workflows:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
